pages.raw_text:
Steve Adams, handcuffer to
Sheriff c. A. Fitzpatrick of Tellu-
ride, Colo, was kept at the Victoria
hotel last night and this morning
was started for Colorado, where he
is to face charge of murder, said
to have been committed by him dur
ing the miners troubles at Cripple
Creek. Attorney Walter Hanson,
of Shoshone, Idaho, also stopped at
the Victoria.


1t was at first expected that
Adams would be extradicted here
by Attorney Fred Miller, represen-
tative Of the Western Federation, of
Miners, but Miller said this MON
ing that he never had any intention
of preventing the officers from TAX
ing Adams to Colorado or any other
place, as when both the juries
which tried him failed to arrive at
verdict. he was through with the
case The Idaho charges against
Adams were dismissed by Judge
Wood yesterday.
